verses.text: Solomon makes the great brazen altar and the molten sea on twelve oxen. Altar and laver — sacrifice and cleansing — stand together. They preach the sinner's two needs in drawing near to God: atonement for guilt and washing from defilement.

Solomon makes the great brazen altar and the molten sea on twelve oxen. Altar and laver — sacrifice and cleansing — stand together. They preach the sinner's two needs in drawing near to God: atonement for guilt and washing from defilement.

Ten lavers, ten golden lampstands, ten tables, and the courts are made. The house is richly supplied for worship — light, bread, and cleansing in abundance. God provides fully for the worship of His people, sparing nothing.

Huram completes the pots, shovels, basins, and all the bronze work for the house of God. Every needed vessel is supplied. The worship of God requires many ordinary instruments, and the faithful craftsman makes them all with care.

Huram completes the pots, shovels, basins, and all the bronze work for the house of God. Every needed vessel is supplied. The worship of God requires many ordinary instruments, and the faithful craftsman makes them all with care.

So much bronze is used that its weight 'could not be found out,' beyond all reckoning. The abundance overflows counting. When hearts give wholly to God's house, the supply runs past measure; lavish devotion does not stop to weigh its gifts.

The golden vessels — the altar, the shewbread tables, the lampstands, the bowls — and the very doors are made of pure gold. The inner house gleams with the purest gold. The nearer to God's presence, the more glorious; He is worthy of our purest best.
